title: High Protein Crockpot Chicken Chili
metaDescription: Set-and-forget crockpot chicken chili with 40g protein per serving. Shredded chicken, white beans and green chiles, 6 hours in the slow cooker.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: crockpot chicken chili, white chicken chili, high protein slow cooker
ingredients: 3 cans white beans, drained and rinsed
1/2 cup plain Greek yogurt, for serving
Shredded cheese and cilantro, for serving

steps.name: Load the crockpot
steps.text: Add chicken breast, white beans, green chiles, chicken broth, onion, garlic, cumin, oregano, and cayenne (if using) to the crockpot. Stir to combine.

steps.name: Slow cook
steps.text: Cover and cook on low for 6 hours, or on high for 3-4 hours, until the chicken shreds easily with a fork.

steps.name: Shred the chicken
steps.text: Remove the chicken breasts, shred with two forks, and return to the crockpot. Stir to combine with the broth and beans.

steps.name: Finish and serve
steps.text: Ladle into bowls and top each with a dollop of Greek yogurt, shredded cheese, and fresh cilantro.

tips.question: Can I use chicken thighs instead of breast?
tips.answer: Yes — boneless, skinless thighs work well and stay slightly more moist, though the protein content per serving will be a touch lower since thighs have more fat.

tips.question: Why add Greek yogurt instead of sour cream?
tips.answer: Greek yogurt adds nearly the same creamy tang as sour cream but with significantly more protein per tablespoon — a simple swap that boosts the final protein count.

tips.question: Can I make this on the stovetop instead?
tips.answer: Yes — simmer everything in a large pot over low heat for about 45 minutes, covered, until the chicken is fully cooked and shreds easily.

tips.question: How long does this keep in the fridge?
tips.answer: Up to 5 days in an airtight container, and it freezes well for up to 3 months — freeze in individual portions for easy grab-and-go lunches.

storyIntro: White chicken chili is the crockpot recipe that quietly does more work than it looks like — dump everything in before work, and six hours later there's a pot of shredded chicken chili with almost no active cooking time.
At 40g of protein per serving, it's one of the highest-protein crockpot meals that doesn't rely on a protein powder shortcut — just chicken, beans, and time.
storyNote: Shred the chicken directly in the crockpot broth rather than on a separate cutting board — it soaks up more flavor and stays moister that way.
